title: Physical Detail
description: Evocative words for a physical or sensory quality of texture, condition, or appearance, each with associations to sharpen description.
entries: WEATHERED — worn, sun-bleached, seasoned by time
GLEAMING — shining, polished, catching the light
JAGGED — ragged, sawtoothed, sharp at the break
SODDEN — soaked, waterlogged, heavy with damp
BRITTLE — fragile, snappable, crumbling at a touch
VELVETY — soft, plush, smooth to the palm
RUSTED — corroded, flaking, orange with decay
TRANSLUCENT — sheer, glowing, letting light through
GRITTY — sandy, coarse, rough on the skin
SUPPLE — pliant, limber, bending without cracking
CRACKED — split, fissured, veined with faults
LUSTROUS — glossy, radiant, deep with sheen
COARSE — rough, bristly, harsh to the grip
PRISTINE — spotless, flawless, untouched and clean
GNARLED — twisted, knotted, warped with age
SLICK — slippery, oiled, greased to the touch
FRAYED — unraveling, threadbare, worn at the edge
IRIDESCENT — shimmering, oil-slick, shifting in hue
CRUMBLING — decaying, powdery, falling to dust
SMOOTH — sleek, unbroken, glass to the hand
SCORCHED — burnt, blackened, seared by fire
DOWNY — fluffy, feathery, light as breath
PITTED — pocked, dented, scarred with holes
GLOSSY — shiny, lacquered, wet-looking sheen
MOSSY — furred, green, soft with growth
TATTERED — shredded, in tatters, torn to ribbons
OPALESCENT — milky, pearl-lit, glowing from within
STICKY — tacky, clinging, gummed to the touch
FROSTED — rimed, iced, dusted white with cold
SINEWY — stringy, taut, roped with fiber
BLOATED — swollen, distended, bulging with excess
CHALKY — dry, powdery, pale and dusty
SPLINTERED — shattered, jagged, bristling with shards
GLASSY — clear, mirror-still, hard and bright
MATTED — tangled, clumped, pressed into knots
SCALY — flaked, plated, rough with overlap
DEWY — moist, glistening, beaded with wet
WITHERED — shriveled, wilted, dried to a husk
BURNISHED — buffed, gleaming, worked to a shine
MUDDY — churned, filthy, clogged with muck
PRICKLY — thorny, spiny, sharp to the reach
MOTTLED — blotched, dappled, patched in tone
PARCHED — bone-dry, cracked, thirsting for rain
PLUSH — cushioned, thick, sinking underhand
TARNISHED — dulled, filmed, faded from its shine
